title: Digital Logic Design: Principles and Applications
textbook_refs: Wakerly: Digital Design Principles and Practices 5th Ed

topics.title: Number Systems: Binary, Octal, Hexadecimal Conversions and BCD
Boolean Algebra Theorems: Duality, De Morgan's Laws, and Consensus Theorem
Canonical Forms: SOP, POS, Minterms, Maxterms, and Conversion
Karnaugh Maps: 2, 3, 4 Variable Simplification and Don't-Care Conditions
Quine–McCluskey Method: Tabular Minimization for Many Variables
Logic Gate Families: TTL, CMOS, ECL, and Electrical Characteristics
CMOS Logic Gate Implementation: Pull-Up/Pull-Down Networks
Half and Full Adders: Ripple Carry, Carry Lookahead, and Carry Select
Decoders, Encoders, and Priority Encoders: Design and Applications
Multiplexers and Demultiplexers: Design, Universal Logic, and Applications
Comparators and Barrel Shifters: Design and VLSI Implementations
SR, D, JK, and T Flip-Flops: Characteristic Tables and Excitation Tables
Registers and Shift Registers: SIPO, PISO, PIPO, and Ring Counters
Synchronous and Asynchronous Counters: Modulo-N and Up/Down Counters
Mealy and Moore Machines: State Minimization and State Assignment
State Diagram to State Table to Logic Circuit: Complete Design Process
Hazards and Timing: Static, Dynamic Hazards, Glitches, and Elimination
Synchronization, Metastability, and Clock Domain Crossing
ROM, RAM, PLA, and PAL: Programmable Logic and Memory Devices
FPGAs and CPLDs: LUT-Based Architecture and HDL Programming Introduction
Verilog HDL Introduction: Behavioral, Dataflow, and Structural Modeling
Testbench Design and Simulation: ModelSim and Waveform Analysis
Placement Guide: Digital Logic Interview Questions and GATE Preparation
